examples.title: Algorithm: Contiguous Allocation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ContiguousAllocation
INPUT: file size, block size
OUTPUT: block addresses

BEGIN
 IF free space available THEN
 allocate contiguous blocks
 update directory entry
 RETURN block addresses
 ELSE
 RETURN error
END

examples.title: Python: Linked Allocation
examples.code:
class Block:
 def __init__(self, address, next=None):
 self.address = address
 self.next = next

class File:
 def __init__(self, name):
 self.name = name
 self.blocks = []

 def add_block(self, address):
 block = Block(address)
 self.blocks.append(block)
 return block

# Create a file and add blocks
file = File('example.txt')
block1 = file.add_block(0x1000)
block2 = file.add_block(0x2000)
block1.next = block2
examples.explanation: Key implementation details of linked allocation
